verdict_reasoning: TMED10 (TMP21/p23) is a type I single-pass transmembrane protein of the p24 family with a cytoplasmic tail, a TM domain, and a luminal GOLD domain. Its canonical residence is the Golgi/ERGIC with cycling to the plasma membrane — the NCBI summary explicitly notes localization to both the plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae. It participates in COPI/COPII vesicle trafficking and cycles between Golgi and PM. A documented PM pool exists (surface biotinylation studies confirm PM presence), qualifying it as dual_localization. The extracellular/luminal GOLD domain is accessible from the outer face when at the PM.
